metadata.title: Why a Perfect AI Model Killed the Doorbell

beats.narration_text: Hola, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. The model was accurate, the hardware worked, the firmware shipped — and the product still died in the field.

props.command: Why did a perfectly accurate AI model drain the doorbell battery in 48 hours instead of 6 months?
output: Inference time × frequency = duty cycle → average current → battery life

beats.narration_text: The textbook puts it in three bars: memory passes, latency passes, power fails. That single red bar is why the doorbell died.

Every AI model on an embedded device has three budgets it has to pass: memory, latency, and power. Memory and latency were fine. Power was the failure. The power bar overshot the target — 1.7 milliamps average draw against a budget that required under 0.65 milliamps to hit six months.

The math starts with duty cycle: inference time divided by the period between inferences. The doorbell was running continuous inference — firmware update accidentally zeroed the period. Duty cycle hit one hundred percent. Eleven percent was the design target.

Battery life is capacity divided by average current. 220 milliamp-hours divided by 1.7 milliamps is 130 hours — five and a half days. The 14-day spec required 336 hours. That terracotta bar falls short by more than half.

The fix is simple: restore periodic inference. Run the model once every 30 seconds, not continuously. That drives duty cycle back to 11 percent, average current to 0.17 milliamps, and battery life to the 14-day target.

Your turn. Paste this into Claude: I have a model that runs in inference-ms milliseconds on my MCU. It runs freq times per second. Active current is active-mA milliamps, sleep current is sleep-µA microamps, battery is mAh milliamp-hours. Compute my duty cycle, average current, and expected battery life. Then: what happens to battery life if I double the inference frequency? Plug in your real numbers — or use the doorbell numbers from this video — and watch what happens when you double the frequency.

props.command: I have a model that runs in [inference_ms] ms on my MCU. It runs [freq] times per second. Active current is [active_mA] mA, sleep current is [sleep_uA] µA, battery is [mAh] mAh. Compute my duty cycle, average current, and expected battery life. Then: what happens to battery life if I double the inference frequency?
